label: 59_arab_tunisia_arab spring_tunisian
description: The arab spring uprisings in tunisia and egypt led to widespread political change across the middle east. scholars and policymakers debated u.s. involvement and the role of democracy promotion, as autocratic regimes fell and new challenges emerged.

earliest_articles.title: Can Obama Erase ‘Bush Nostalgia’ in the Middle East?
earliest_articles.excerpt: While President Obama’s domestic position has been strengthened considerably by the passage of health-care reform, there is nothing – yet – to suggest global support for American foreign policy will follow suit. Outside the United States, there is a sense of “Bush nostalgia,” including in a rather unlikely place – the…

earliest_articles.title: المجتمع المدني في العالم العربي و معضلة التمويل
earliest_articles.excerpt: لعب المجتمع المدني دوراً حاسماً في عمليات التحول الديمقراطي في أوروبا الشرقية و أمريكا اللاتينية؛ لكنه لو يؤد المهمة نفسها في العالم العربي، و هذا ما يعود على كثيرين بالإحباط و خيبة الأمل. و في الواقع تعرضت المنظمات الأهلية غير الحكومية للإضعاف و التدجين في أنحاء المنطقة كلها. و في غضون ذلك، لا تتوقف الولايات المتحدة…

earliest_articles.title: Civil Society in the Arab World and the Dilemma of Funding
earliest_articles.excerpt: Civil society played a critical role in democratic transitions in Eastern Europe and Latin America. To the disappointment of many, it hasn’t played that role in the Arab world. Across the region, non-governmental organizations have been weakened and tamed. Meanwhile, the United States, the EU, and the international…

newest_articles.title: S. 2006, Safeguarding Tunisian Democracy Act of 2023
newest_articles.excerpt: S. 2006 would authorize the appropriation of $100 million each year for 2024 and 2025 to the Department of State for programs that support reforms to restore democratic institutions and the rule of law in Tunisia and that stabilize the Tunisian economy. That assistance would not be available for obligation until the…

newest_articles.title: It’s not too late: How to save Tunisian democracy
newest_articles.excerpt: Tunisian democracy is dying. If there was ever any doubt, the last few months have revealed to all that President Kais Saied’s moves on July 25, 2021, were a coup d’état. Saied has seized all powers, arrested journalists and dissidents of all stripes, and incited violence against migrants and black Tunisians. Tunisia…

central_articles.title: The Struggle for Middle East Democracy
central_articles.excerpt: It always seemed as if Arab countries were ‘on the brink.’ It turns out that they were. And those who assured us that Arab autocracies would last for decades, if not longer, were wrong. In the wake of the Tunisian and Egyptian revolutions, academics, analysts and certainly Western policymakers must reassess their…

central_articles.title: What Obama and American Liberals Don’t Understand About the Arab Spring
central_articles.excerpt: Throughout the Arab spring, analysts and policymakers have debated the proper role that the United States should be playing in the Middle East.
